remaining_gap: Retry packet handling, token issuance/replay, and PTO/send-path behavior remain blocked by missing connection-state, timer, and token-cryptography surfaces.
notes: No reconciliation artifacts existed for this chunk, so it was treated as greenfield. The implemented slice covers address validation and anti-amplification budgeting; the remaining requirements need connection-state and token-lifecycle machinery that is not present yet.

requirements.notes: The anti-amplification budget helper now enforces the three-times cap until validation, and the tests cover both the capped and post-validation paths.
The helper implements the endpoint-chosen CID plus 64-bit structural check that this repo can express without a full entropy-analysis surface.
Servers are limited to three times the payload bytes counted by the helper before address validation completes.
Only datagrams uniquely attributable to a single connection contribute to the received-byte tally.
Initial datagram padding is formatted as repeated PADDING frames until the 1200-byte minimum is reached.

requirements.remaining_gap: PTO and packet-probe scheduling are not wired into any send-path or timer state, so there is nowhere to emit the required probe packet.
requirements.notes: This is blocked by the missing recovery/timer surface rather than by the address-validation helpers added in this chunk.

requirements.remaining_gap: The transmit path does not yet know whether Handshake keys are available, so it cannot choose between Initial and Handshake packet emission.
requirements.notes: This depends on connection-state and key-availability logic that is not present in the current codebase slice.

requirements.remaining_gap: PTO and packet-probe scheduling are not wired into any send-path or timer state, so the deadlock-avoidance probe packet cannot be emitted.
requirements.notes: This remains blocked on the same recovery/timer machinery as REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S8P1-0005.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no token envelope or provenance metadata surface yet to distinguish tokens issued in NEW_TOKEN frames from tokens carried in Retry packets.
requirements.notes: The current frame and packet helpers do not yet model token construction or token provenance.

requirements.remaining_gap: Retry packet parsing/formatting and the client Initial-token replay path are missing, so Retry-token repetition and validation cannot be implemented yet.
requirements.notes: This chunk still lacks the Retry-specific packet model and the state needed to carry a token across connection attempts.

requirements.remaining_gap: There is no token issuance, storage, expiry, applicability, or replay-management surface yet for NEW_TOKEN or server-selected address-validation tokens.
requirements.notes: All token lifecycle and validation behavior in Section 8.1.3 depends on missing connection-state and server token management infrastructure.
